history.notes: Cold plunge/cold exposure/Wim Hof trend 80M+ TikTok views. Session timer + streak tracker + temperature logging. ASO: popularity 30, difficulty 15.
Moved to researching — spawning validator agent

GO: Growing $330M+ cold plunge market with fragmented app competition (top standalone tracker has only 254 ratings), low ASO difficulty 15, and straightforward SwiftUI build with no expensive APIs or App Store risk.

Posted to 4/5 platforms (TikTok, Instagram, X, Threads). YouTube Shorts skipped — requires video attachment, no video assets available. Assets at ~/moss/content/cold-plunge-log/. All posts via Postiz.
4 platforms posted (>= 3 threshold). Advancing to content-tracking for social validation monitoring.
Reset for repost — old content deleted, Content Creator rewritten

demandSignals: Cold plunge tub market valued at $330-390M in 2024-2025, growing at 6.5-8.1% CAGR to $660-730M by 2033 (Grand View Research, SkyQuest, Future Market Insights)
TikTok cold plunge content at 80M+ combined views; mainstream adoption expanding beyond athletes into general wellness
r/BecomingTheIceman ~10K subscribers, r/coldshowers ~13K subscribers — active communities discussing cold exposure routines and tracking needs
Wim Hof Method app has 11,499 App Store ratings showing massive engaged community around cold exposure and breathwork
Multiple market research firms confirm cold exposure trend is growing driven by influencer endorsements, athlete adoption, and wellness culture expansion
marketSize.notes: Cold exposure has crossed from niche biohacker trend into mainstream wellness. Hardware market growth (tubs, plunges) creates derivative demand for software tracking. The Wim Hof app's 11K+ ratings prove willingness to download cold-exposure-related apps.

topApps.name: Plunge (Official)
topApps.notes: Hardware companion app for Plunge brand tubs — not a standalone tracker. Free because it drives hardware sales.

topApps.name: GoPolar: Cold Plunge & Sauna
topApps.notes: $6.99/mo or $39.99/yr. Tracks cold plunge and sauna. Complaints about manual sauna data entry and limited analytics.

topApps.name: Brisk: Cold Plunge Tracker
topApps.notes: $4.99/mo or $39.99/yr. Session tracking with health metrics. Small user base.

topApps.name: Ultra Plunge: Cold & Sauna
topApps.notes: $4.99/mo or $39.99/yr. AI coaching features. 7-day free trial. Lower rating suggests quality gaps.

topApps.name: IceBuddy: Cold Plunge Tracker
topApps.notes: Newest entrant. Apple Watch support. Still rolling out paid features.

topApps.name: Cold Water Therapy
topApps.notes: $2.99/mo or $22.99/yr. Poor rating indicates low quality — opportunity to capture disappointed users.

topApps.name: Wim Hof Method
topApps.notes: $5.99/mo or $42.99/yr. Breathwork-focused, not a plunge tracker. Adjacent but different core value prop.

competitors.identifiedGap: No competitor combines a polished breathing timer + temperature logging + shareable stats cards + streak tracking in one focused cold plunge app. Plunge is hardware-locked. GoPolar splits focus with sauna. Wim Hof is breathwork-centric. Reviews cite manual data entry friction and wanting better analytics/sharing features.
competitors.notes: Only 6 direct competitors with the top standalone tracker (GoPolar) at just 254 ratings. This is an undersaturated category with clear room for a well-designed entrant. The Plunge app has traction but only serves Plunge hardware owners.

monetization.mrrMath: Conservative: 500 downloads/mo * 5% conversion * $4.99 = $125/mo in month 1. With content marketing growth to 2,000 downloads/mo by month 3: 2,000 * 5% * $4.99 = $499/mo. Cumulative subscribers compound. Reaching $500-1,000 MRR by month 3 is plausible. Note: original $1.99/week ($8.67/mo) pricing is above market — recommend $4.99/mo to match competitors.
monetization.notes: Subscription model is validated by all competitors using it. $4.99/mo is the market sweet spot. The $1.99/week price from the scout brief should be adjusted down to $4.99/mo or $29.99/yr to match category norms and reduce subscription fatigue. Annual plan at $29.99 provides good LTV.

technicalFeasibility.notes: Pure SwiftUI build: countdown timer, temperature number input, streak logic with UserDefaults/SwiftData, Swift Charts for history, ShareLink for stats cards (rendered as images), breathing timer with simple animation. HealthKit integration for workout logging is a standard API. No backend needed for MVP — local storage with optional CloudKit sync. Apple Watch companion would differentiate but is not required for v1. No expensive APIs, no rate-limited services, no complex backend.

concerns: Must avoid making specific health benefit claims in App Store metadata (e.g., 'reduces inflammation', 'boosts immune system') — frame as tracking/logging only
HealthKit usage requires NSHealthShareUsageDescription and NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription privacy strings
appStoreRisk.notes: This is a tracking/logging app, not a medical device or health advisor. Multiple competitors are already approved in this exact category. No regulated category concerns (not medical, financial, or kids). Standard HealthKit permissions are well-understood by App Store review. Low rejection probability.
